quotes.text: It is not unreasonable that we grapple with problems. [...] Our responsibility is to do what we can, learn what we can, improve the solutions, and pass them on.
quotes.author: Richard P. Feynman
quotes.source: The Pleasure of Finding Things Out

quotes.text: Discomfort is the price of admission to a meaningful life.
quotes.author: Susan David
quotes.source: TED Talk - The gift and power of emotional courage

quotes.text: I have no special talents. I am only passionately curious.
quotes.author: Albert Einstein

quotes.text: Nothing in life is to be feared, it is only to be understood. Now is the time to understand more, so that we may fear less.
quotes.author: Marie Curie

quotes.text: Simplicity is prerequisite for reliability.
quotes.author: Edsger W. Dijkstra
quotes.source: EWD 498 - How do we tell truths that might hurt? (1975)

quotes.text: Programs must be written for people to read, and only incidentally for machines to execute.
quotes.author: Harold Abelson and Gerald Jay Sussman
quotes.source: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs (1984)

quotes.text: It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it.
quotes.author: Aristotle

quotes.text: The fundamental cause of the trouble is that in the modern world the stupid are cocksure while the intelligent are full of doubt.
quotes.author: Bertrand Russell

quotes.text: You are not controlling the storm, and you are not lost in it. You are the storm.
quotes.author: Sam Harris

quotes.text: You can do what you decide to do — but you cannot decide what you will decide to do.
quotes.author: Sam Harris

quotes.text: What can be asserted without evidence can also be dismissed without evidence.
quotes.author: Christopher Hitchens

quotes.text: The idea that the future is unpredictable is undermined every day by the ease with which the past is explained.
quotes.author: Daniel Kahneman

quotes.text: Repeat after me: pharma being shit does not mean magic beans cure cancer.
quotes.author: Ben Goldacre

quotes.text: Throughout history, every mystery ever solved has turned out to be - not magic.
quotes.author: Tim Minchin

quotes.text: A famous bon mot asserts that opinions are like arseholes, in that everyone has one. There is great wisdom in this... but I would add that opinions differ significantly from arseholes, in that yours should be constantly and thoroughly examined.
quotes.author: Tim Minchin

quotes.text: The first principle is that you must not fool yourself — and you are the easiest person to fool.
quotes.author: Richard P. Feynman

quotes.text: Nirvana is where you are, provided you don't object to it.
quotes.author: Alan Watts

quotes.text: A king's lot: to do good and be damned.
quotes.author: Marcus Aurelius

quotes.text: Everything is vague to a degree you do not realize till you have tried to make it precise.
quotes.author: Bertrand Russell

quotes.text: Beware of bugs in the above code; I have only proved it correct, not tried it.
quotes.author: Donald Knuth
quotes.source: Memo to Peter van Emde Boas (1977)

quotes.text: In the end, to worry about life's meaning is to mistake a psychological problem for a philosophical one.
quotes.author: Sam Harris

quotes.text: The reward for good time management is just more work.
quotes.author: Oliver Burkeman
quotes.source: Four Thousand Weeks: Time Management for Mortals (2021)

quotes.text: It turns out that style matters in programming for the same reason that it matters in writing. It makes for better reading.
quotes.author: Douglas Crockford

quotes.text: It is a cruel jest to say to a bootless man that he ought to lift himself by his own bootstraps.
quotes.author: Martin Luther King Jr.
quotes.source: Remaining Awake Through a Great Revolution, National Cathedral sermon (1968)

quotes.text: We are not rational enough to be exposed to the press.
quotes.author: Rutger Bregman
